headline: Apple’s India Production Grows As German Antitrust Risks Test Valuation
summary: Apple (NasdaqGS:AAPL) now produces about 25% of its global iPhone volume in India as part of a shift away from reliance on China. The move reflects supply chain risk management and responses to tariffs tied to China, while opening up manufacturing in a major emerging market. In parallel, Apple faces new antitrust scrutiny in Germany over its app tracking rules, despite proposing changes to its App Tracking Transparency framework. German publishers and advertising groups are urging regulators...

headline: U.S. Data Center Construction Market Outlook Report 2026-2031 Featuring Key Investors - Apple, Applied Digital, AWS, CyrusOne, DataBank, Digital Realty, Equinix, Google, Meta, Microsoft, NTT, Vantage
summary: The U.S. Data Center Construction Market is set to grow from USD 83.97 billion in 2025 to USD 154.49 billion by 2031, accelerating at a CAGR of 10.70%. Key trends driving this expansion include the transformative role of AI in expanding hyperscale data centers, with AI workloads expected to triple capacity within five to six years. headline: DriveWealth Powers Ualá’s Launch of U.S. Stock Investing in Mexico
summary: NEW YORK, March 11, 2026--DriveWealth, a global B2B Brokerage-as-a-Service platform designed to make investing easier for partners and their customers, today announced its partnership with Banco Ualá, a leading Latin American neobank, to launch Acciones (Stocks), a new service enabling Mexican consumers to invest in U.S. equities.

content.title: DriveWealth Powers Ualá’s Launch of U.S. Stock Investing in Mexico
content.summary: NEW YORK, March 11, 2026--DriveWealth, a global B2B Brokerage-as-a-Service platform designed to make investing easier for partners and their customers, today announced its partnership with Banco Ualá, a leading Latin American neobank, to launch Acciones (Stocks), a new service enabling Mexican consumers to invest in U.S. equities.

headline: Apple Smashes India Production Records As Tim Cook Races To Dodge Trillion-Dollar China Tariffs
summary: Apple Inc is rapidly expanding its footprint in India, boosting iPhone production, exploring local chip partnerships, and preparing to launch Apple Pay in one of the world's fastest-growing technology markets. iPhone Production In India Surges According to Bloomberg, Apple increased iPhone production in India by about 53% last year and assembled around 55 million devices in 2025. headline: ETFs With Yields Above 10% Using Completely Different Strategies
summary: With the federal funds rate sitting at 3.75% and the 10-year Treasury yielding 4.13%, investors hunting for meaningful income have to venture well beyond government bonds. Three ETFs currently delivering above 10% yields do so through mechanisms that have almost nothing in common with each other, which makes them worth understanding on their own terms ... headline: The Drone Revolution's Dependence on Chinese Rare Earth Processing - OilPrice.com Market Commentary
summary: Ukraine produced 1.2 million drones in 2024 alone. The scale is significant, as Ukraine is now deploying roughly 9,000 drones per day. But all of those drones share one vulnerability: virtually every magnet in the Ukrainian drones used in 2024 was manufactured in China. And the same is true for Western defense systems across the board. Companies mentioned in this release include: REalloys Inc. (ALOY), Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL), Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO), Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: A

summary: Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) shares are down roughly 21% year-to-date, trading between $134 and $135 intraday on Wednesday. headline: Tim Cook says late Apple cofounder Steve Jobs gave him this unforgettable advice before handing over the reins as CEO: ‘Never ask what I would do’
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook says the advice from Steve Jobs was a “gift” in leading the $3.83 trillion tech giant: “I just put my head down and thought, ‘I’m going to be the best version of myself.”

content.title: Tim Cook says late Apple cofounder Steve Jobs gave him this unforgettable advice before handing over the reins as CEO: ‘Never ask what I would do’
